quote_of_day.text:
>>12326
I use the radio occasionally for what it’s worth. But it highlights how jarring the dissonance between people like you and the rest of the site just is. You don’t really have anything even vaguely in common with the user base of this site.
quote_of_day.name: Ozymandias

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of existential dread hovered over the board, as Anon grappled with questions of identity and purpose.

The thread 'Inch's Identity Crisis: What Does the Board Stand For?' drew a murmur of replies, with one anon bemoaning the lack of a clear ideology.

A scattered front of caste-based insults moved across the board, bringing with it a flurry of slurs and personal attacks. This was accompanied by a brief but intense downpour of fantasies about historical figures, specifically the Mughal reaction to Hindu rituals.

Meanwhile, a series of demands for censorship swept through the board, with one anon calling for a ban on posts about a BJP MLA's daughter and another mocking the 'cucked' status of BJP men.

These threads brought a flurry of replies, but ultimately dissipated without resolution.

articles.title: Inch's Identity Crisis: What Does the Board Stand For?
articles.body:
Anon started a thread on June 14, 2026, questioning the ideology of Indiachan, a small Indian imageboard. The OP wondered what Inch represents, comparing it to other imageboards like 4chan and 2chan.

One respondent, Anon, expressed frustration with the board's current state, calling it a "bland and boring" platform with "no discussion" and "low-quality posts." Another anon suggested that Inch's ideology could be a "critical Nationalist" perspective, where users can criticize India because they love it, but outsiders should not be allowed to do so.

Some users proposed that Inch should take a firm stance against certain groups, such as Muslims, and promote conspiracy theories about Jews.

One anon even suggested that the board should be anti-Semitic and spread conspiracy theories about Jews being responsible for India's problems. However, not all users agreed on the direction the board should take.

Some, like Ozymandias, pointed out the dissonance between users like Kngboz, who wants to preserve the site's resources, and the rest of the user base.

Others, like Anon, questioned the point of doing free work for a site that might shut down in a few months.

The discussion also touched on the idea of "shilling" the site to the world and creating a movement to flood other imageboards with troll posts.

One anon proposed using a Bangladeshi IP to access a Bangladeshi imageboard and spam it with troll posts.

As the conversation continued, users debated the merits of taking a stance against certain groups and the importance of preserving the site's resources.

Despite the disagreements, one thing was clear: the users of Indiachan are struggling to define what their community stands for and where it is headed.

articles.title: Anon exchanges insults, caste slurs
articles.body:
Anon sparked a heated exchange, accusing another poster of being a "pajeet" and a "chamar bastard." The original post was quickly responded to with another Anon suggesting the original poster was "miserable" and only on the board to "laugh at how delusional most posters are." The conversation quickly devolved into personal attacks and insults.

One Anon called another a "gandagi ka dukaan" and a "chamaar bastard." Another Anon responded by telling someone to "jaa saste Vivo wale phone pe free fire khel" and that they were "nalayak saala." The discussion also touched on caste, with one Anon mentioning "Rajchuts" and "lindu n hiban auratans." Another Anon responded with a comment about "Zagros Rajputs > Steppe rape victim of ivc males jhatoras." The tone of the conversation was largely aggressive and mocking, with several posters engaging in name-calling and insults.

At one point, an Anon said "we're done here lmao. stay mad pajeet."

articles.title: Anon Fantasizes Mughal Reaction to Hindu Rituals
articles.body:
Anon posted a speculative scenario imagining the Mughals' reaction to Hindu rituals, suggesting they would have enjoyed and reproduced with participants in 'rajchutya devi dasis' ceremonies.

Another anon responded with a request to moderators to ban a user referred to as 'this rajchutni mughal dasi rand', indicating a desire to prevent further discussion or worship of the individual.

A separate anon expressed frustration and a desire for action against an unnamed entity described as taking 'our tax money', implying dissatisfaction with perceived corruption or misuse of funds.

One early response simply read 'coomed', which may suggest an element of dark humor or absurdity injected into the conversation.

articles.title: Anon demands ban on posts about BJP MLA's daughter
articles.body:
Anon called for the deletion of posts and a ban on a BJP MLA's daughter, citing frustration with repeated appearances of the topic.

Another anon responded, suggesting the daughter was involved with someone described as a Pakistani asset.

Anon also joked about the situation, with one saying 'Rota reh hindu, imagine seething about someone living thousands of mile away and caring about what they eat or what religion guys they fuck.' One respondent suggested violence, saying 'acid attack is highly appropriate in this case'.

The thread drew various reactions, including calls for moderation and dismissal of the topic.

articles.title: BJP men's 'cucked' by daughters meme
articles.body: Anon claimed that the average life of a BJP man is getting 'cucked' by their own daughters. Other anons responded with unsubstantiated claims about BJP politicians' daughters and their personal lives. One anon stated that some BJP politicians' daughters 'suck Pakistani dick, some suck Jihadi dick, some suck White dick and some suck nigger dick'. The discussion included various derogatory terms and personal attacks.
